title: Trouble intensifies for Anil Ambani? SEBI rejects settlement plea in Reliance Infrastructure case
description: SEBI has rejected settlement applications filed by Anil Ambani and his group in an alleged fund diversion case, paving the way for the regulator's investigation to continue while the group denies any wrongdoing.
content: India's capital markets regulator has rejected settlement applications filed by industrialist Anil Ambani and companies linked to his business group in a case involving the alleged diversion of company funds.Securities and Exchange Board of India (SEBI) has decided not to settle the matter, meaning the investigation will continue through the regulatory process. The case relates to allegations that funds from Reliance Infrastructure were routed through another company before reaching businesses linked to the Reliance ADA Group.The Ambani Group has denied the allegations and said it will continue to defend its position through legal channels.What SEBI Is InvestigatingAccording to regulatory documents reviewed by Reuters, SEBI is examining transactions involving Reliance Infrastructure and engineering contractor CLE Private Ltd.The regulator believes company funds were moved through CLE before being invested in businesses associated with the Reliance ADA Group. SEBI has questioned whether these transactions served the interests of public shareholders or were carried out for the benefit of related entities.The regulator has alleged that Reliance Infrastructure diverted around Rs 176.7 billion to CLE over several years. It further claims that at least Rs 112 billion of this amount was invested in companies connected to the Ambani-led group.SEBI has also questioned the company's earlier position that CLE was an independent entity, saying its investigation suggests the company was effectively operating within the Reliance ADA Group's network.The settlement request was rejectedUnder SEBI's settlement mechanism, companies can seek to resolve regulatory proceedings by paying a settlement amount without admitting any wrongdoing.However, documents reviewed by Reuters show that SEBI rejected the applications after taking into account parallel investigations by other enforcement agencies. The regulator has not yet issued its final order in the matter. the investigation is completed, SEBI could impose monetary penalties, market restrictions or other regulatory action if it concludes that securities laws were violated. Any final order can be challenged before the appropriate appellate authority.Ambani Group RespondsResponding to the developments, a spokesperson for the Anil Ambani Group said the allegations are "categorically denied."The spokesperson added that the matters are currently before the courts and that the group will continue to defend its position based on legal advice. This is not the first time SEBI has declined a settlement request from Anil Ambani.Last year, the regulator also rejected a settlement application linked to a separate case involving investments in Yes Bank.The development comes as Reliance Infrastructure is preparing to raise fresh capital from investors. The company's board has approved plans to raise up to Rs 30 billion through the market to support its business and future growth.The outcome of SEBI's investigation could be closely watched by investors as the fundraising process moves ahead.What Happens NextThe rejection of the settlement request does not amount to a final finding of guilt. It means the regulatory proceedings will continue and SEBI is expected to issue a detailed order after completing its investigation.Until then, the allegations remain under examination, while the company maintains that it has done no wrong and will contest the case through legal proceedings.

title: পুনৰ জামিন নাকচ সিদ্ধাৰ্থ শৰ্মাৰ...
Why did Vaibhav Sooryavanshi not make his debut for India in 1st T20I against Ireland?
description: Against the expectations of fans, 15-year-old Vaibhav Sooryavanshi did not get a spot in the playing XI for the 1st T20I against Ireland.
content: The new season is underway with the 1st T20I between India and Ireland. The match was expected to go down in history, as speculations about 15-year-old Vaibhav Sooryavanshi making his debut were rife ahead of the game. However, the teenage sensation would have to wait a little longer to make his first impression on the international circuit, as he did not find a spot in the playing XI of the match.Ireland vs India Live Streaming: How to watch the 1st T20I in India, USA, UK, Australia, Afghanistan and IrelandWhy did Vaibhav Sooryavanshi not make his debut in the 1st T20I vs Ireland?The new captain of India's T20 team, Shreyas Iyer, revealed at the toss that Sooryavanshi is not a part of the team for today's game. Iyer acknowledged the talent of Sooryavanshi but preferred to go with a balanced XI."We have tremendous experience at the top. He will get his opportunity in due course. For now, we're going with three genuine seamers and one all-rounder," Iyer said at the toss.India's playing XI: India's playing XI: Samson, Abhishek, Kishan, Iyer (C), Tilak, Dube, Axar, Sundar, Rana, Arshdeep and Prasidh.India opt to bowlLuck favoured India's new captain, Shreyas Iyer, at the toss, and he opted to bowl first. The visitors started on a brilliant note, putting Ireland in a troublesome position at 51/4 inside eight overs. However, the wicketkeeper batter Lorcan Tucker got things going for Ireland. He registered a solid fifty with the help of five boundaries and a couple of sixes.Tucker's innings brought positivity to Ireland's side as they are on the cusp of posting a challenging total on the board. As per the recent update, Gareth Delany (37* off 24) and George Dockrell (19 off 9) are currently in the middle, and Ireland are 164/5 after 17 overs, heading toward the 200 mark.

title: NSE Chief asks startups, MSMEs to consider Stock Market listing for long-term growth - DETAILS
description: NSE CEO Ashish Chauhan urged businesses to prioritise profitability, innovation and long-term growth over short-term share price movements, saying strong business fundamentals ultimately drive market valuation and investor confidence.
content: National Stock Exchange (NSE) Managing Director and CEO Ashish Chauhan has urged companies to focus on strengthening their businesses, improving profitability and creating sustainable long-term value instead of becoming overly concerned about day-to-day movements in their share prices.Speaking at the 9th JITO Incubation & Innovation Foundation (JIIF) Day event on Friday, Chauhan said a company's market valuation should naturally reflect the strength of its business and financial performance.He advised entrepreneurs to concentrate on expanding their businesses and delivering greater value to shareholders through strong fundamentals rather than trying to influence stock prices."If the company's profits grow, its share price will eventually follow. It is not possible to keep increasing market value without genuine business growth," Chauhan said.He also encouraged founders to remain committed to their business goals instead of getting distracted by market sentiment or comparisons with competitors. According to him, companies that consistently deliver growth and strong financial performance ultimately earn recognition from investors.Highlighting the advantages of public markets, Chauhan said listed companies often enjoy valuations that private businesses cannot easily achieve. He explained that a company earning an annual profit of around Rs 2 crore could potentially command a market capitalisation of Rs 40 crore to Rs 50 crore after listing, giving promoters greater flexibility to raise funds, attract strategic partners and expand operations.Chauhan further said that being listed provides companies with an additional strategic advantage, as their shares can be used as a currency for acquisitions, partnerships and employee compensation through stock options. He cited Infosys founders N.R. Narayana Murthy and Nandan Nilekani, who introduced employee stock ownership plans (ESOPs) in the company's early years to attract talented professionals despite limited financial resources.Speaking about entrepreneurship, Chauhan stressed that innovation is not limited to breakthrough technologies. Even small improvements in products, services or business processes can drive meaningful innovation."Whatever you do, if you do it differently and better, that is also innovation," he said.He acknowledged that building a successful business requires patience and perseverance, with many entrepreneurs spending years overcoming challenges before achieving meaningful success.On the topic of stock market listings, Chauhan said the listing process is often perceived as more difficult than it actually is. However, he emphasised that companies must remain committed to good governance, regulatory compliance and transparency after entering the public markets.Concluding his address, Chauhan said capital markets provide businesses with significant opportunities to raise funds and accelerate growth, but long-term success depends on maintaining financial discipline and consistently creating value for shareholders.

title: Nashik News : नाशिकमध्ये २८ जूनला ‘मिशन ग्रीन मान्सून’ अभियानाचा शुभारंभ
description: नाशिक । जिमाका वृत्तसेवा Nashik प्रशासन, स्थानिक स्वराज्य संस्था, स्वयंसेवी संस्था, उद्योग, शैक्षणिक संस्था आणि नागरिक यांच्या सक्रिय लोकसहभागातून हरित नाशिक साकारण्याचा संकल्प करण्यात आला असून, या अभियानाचा शुभारंभ २८ जून रोजी कुंभमेळा मंत्री गिरीश महाजन यांच्या उपस्थितीत होणार आहे. या अभियानात प्रत्येक नाशिककराने सक्रिय सहभाग नोंदवावा, असे आवाहन जिल्हाधिकारी आयुष प्रसाद यांनी केले आहे. […]
content:
नाशिक । जिमाका वृत्तसेवा Nashik



प्रशासन, स्थानिक स्वराज्य संस्था, स्वयंसेवी संस्था, उद्योग, शैक्षणिक संस्था आणि नागरिक यांच्या सक्रिय लोकसहभागातून हरित नाशिक साकारण्याचा संकल्प करण्यात आला असून, या अभियानाचा शुभारंभ २८ जून रोजी कुंभमेळा मंत्री गिरीश महाजन यांच्या उपस्थितीत होणार आहे. या अभियानात प्रत्येक नाशिककराने सक्रिय सहभाग नोंदवावा, असे आवाहन जिल्हाधिकारी आयुष प्रसाद यांनी केले आहे.



जिल्हाधिकारी कार्यालयातील नियेाजन सभागृहात ‘मिशन ग्रीन मान्सून’ अभियानाच्या नियोजनासाठी आयोजित जिल्हास्तरीय समन्वय बैठकीत ते बोलत होते. यावेळी उपजिल्हाधिकारी सीमा आहिरे, उपवनसंरक्षक सिद्धेश सावर्डेकर, मंत्री गिरीश महाजन यांचे विशेष कार्य अधिकारी संदीप जाधव, वनाधिकारी रणदिवे, मनपा उद्यान निरीक्षक विवेक भदाने, वृक्षमित्र शेखर गायकवाड, चंदू पाटील,जगबिर सिंग,मनोज साठे,वैभव देशमुख,मनीष बाविस्कर,भारती जाधव,राम खुर्दळ,भारत पिंगळे,शिवाजी धोंडगे,सागर बैरागी,गौरव देशमुख, सोनल दगडे यांच्यासह शहरातील पर्यावरण प्रेमी उपस्थित होते.



जिल्हाधिकारी प्रसाद म्हणाले की, सातपूर एमआयडीसी परिसरात असलेल्या देवराई ( फाशीचा डोंगर) या ठिकाणी वृक्षमित्र शेखर गायकवाड यांच्या माध्यमातून झाडांची लागवड करण्यात आली आहे, आजही या परिसरात वृक्ष लागवडीचे काम सुरू आहे. 28 जून 2026 रोजी कुंभमेळा मंत्री गिरीश महाजन यांच्या उपस्थितीत मिशन ग्रीन मान्सून अंतर्गत देखील दोन हजार झाडांचे रोपण या ठिकाणी करण्यात येणार आहे. त्यासोबतच शिंदे ग्रामपंचायतचे सरपंच बाजीराव जाधव यांनी देखील दोन हजार वृक्ष लागवडीचा संकल्प केला असून 28 जून रोजी ग्रामपंचायत परिसरात दोन हजार वृक्ष लागवड करण्यात येणार आहे. तसेच शहरातील विविध ठिकाणावरून वृक्ष लागवडीसाठी अनेक नागरिकांनी मागणी केलेली आहे, या जागेवर देखील महानगरपिालका प्रशासनामार्फत रोपे पुरवण्यात येणार आहे. अशाप्रकारे सुमारे पाच ते सहा हजार वृक्ष लागवडीचे उद्दिष्ट येत्या रविवारी सुरू होणाऱ्या मिशन ग्रीन मान्सून च्या माध्यमातून ठेवण्यात आले असल्याचे त्यांनी सांगितले.



“महानगरपालिका व परिसरात सार्वजनिक ठिकाणी वृक्ष लागवडीसाठी योग्य जागा उपलब्ध असेल अशा परिसरातून नागरिकांनी देखील मागणी केली तर त्यांना झाडे उपलब्ध करून दिले जाणारआहेत. याकरिता सोबतचा QR कोड स्कॅन करून आपली मागणी नोंदवायची आहे. जागा सुयोग्य असल्यास प्रशासनामार्फत या ठिकाणी मोफत झाडे पुरवली जाणार आहेत, तरी या उपक्रमात नागरिकांनी मोठ्या प्रमाणावर सहभाग घेऊन हरित नाशिक चळवळीत सहभाग घ्यावा–कुंभमेळा मंत्री गिरीश महाजन

title: Shank Mitra Networth: Indian-origin CEO become world’s second-highest-paid executive, just behind Elon Musk
description: Indian-origin Welltower CEO Shankh Mitra has emerged as the world's second-highest paid executive in 2025, receiving an USD 821 million compensation package largely linked to long-term stock-based incentives.
content: Shankh Mitra, the Indian-origin CEO of Welltower Inc., emerged as the world’s second-highest-paid executive, earning a staggering USD 821 million second only to Elon Musk. The revelation, part of The Wall Street Journal’s annual CEO compensation ranking, has spotlighted the quiet but powerful rise of Indian talent in global business. Man Behind the NumbersBorn and educated in India, Mitra graduated with a Bachelor of Engineering in Instrumentation and Electronics from Kolkata’s Jadavpur University. He later earned an MBA from Columbia Business School, specializing in applied value investing. His professional journey began at PwC, followed by key roles at Fidelity Investments, Citadel, and Millennium Management, where he sharpened his expertise in real estate securities and investment strategy.In 2016, Mitra joined Welltower, a leading S&P 500 real estate investment trust (REIT) focused on senior housing, healthcare facilities, and life sciences properties. His rapid ascent saw him become Chief Investment Officer in 2018 and CEO in October 2020. Under his leadership, Welltower’s stock has more than tripled, pushing the company’s market capitalisation past USD 100 billion by capitalising on the booming demand driven by aging populations.The eye-popping USD 821 million pay package for 2025 stems from a bold 10-year executive compensation plan. Mitra and top executives opted for massive equity-linked incentives tied to long-term performance rather than traditional salary and bonuses. While the headline number is enormous, a significant portion vests only if Welltower delivers sustained shareholder returns over the decade.This performance-driven structure reflects Mitra’s investment philosophy of deep alignment with long-term value creation. However, it has drawn mixed reactions from shareholders, with the “Say-on-Pay” vote receiving only 19 per cent support. Mitra’s success adds to the growing list of Indian-origin leaders dominating global corporate ranks from tech giants like Sundar Pichai and Satya Nadella to finance and real estate visionaries. His story highlights how strong engineering foundations, rigorous financial training, and domain expertise can create extraordinary outcomes in competitive American markets.Beyond numbers, Mitra is regarded as an analytical, puzzle-solving leader who has strategically positioned Welltower at the intersection of real estate and healthcare innovation. As demographic shifts continue to favour senior living and medical infrastructure, his vision appears well-timed.Shankh Mitra’s journey from Jadavpur classrooms to commanding one of the richest paychecks in corporate America is more than a compensation story. It represents the triumph of merit, strategic thinking, and long-term conviction in a high-stakes global economy. As he steers Welltower through the next decade, the world will watch whether this massive bet translates into enduring value for investors and cements his legacy among the finest business leaders of his generation.

title: SpaceX plans to launch Starlink mobile service in the US
description: Move would test whether group can turn ambition into a mass-market phone business.
content:
Elon Musk’s SpaceX has told investors that it plans to launch a new Starlink mobile service for US consumers, in a move that would upend the country’s multibillion-dollar phone network market.
The company’s president and chief operating officer, Gwynne Shotwell, told investors during a recent IPO roadshow that the group was considering launching a Starlink retail product and could build its own terrestrial US mobile network, according to four people familiar with the matter.
The move would require Starlink to build a new retail offering by selling mobile contracts to individual customers, competing directly with the three big US network operators Verizon Wireless, AT&T. and T-Mobile.Read full article

title: Ishita Moitra: Bringing contemporary female gaze into mainstream narratives
description: There is a scene in almost every Ishita Moitra story where people take time to reveal who they really are. A woman says something honest over drinks. A family argument suddenly turns tender. A joke hides a heartbreak. A glamorous world exposes its hidden fragments.Across television, OTT and cinema, Moitra’s writing stands on these human contradictions. Her women are not perfect, rarely symbolic, and almost never written to fit stereotypes. They are ambitious and nurturing, insecure and self-aware, funny and vulnerable, often within the same scene.That sense of storyteller has made Moitra one of the most distinctive writing voices in the film industry. From Four More Shots Please! and The Test Case to Rocky Aur Rani Kii Prem Kahaani, her storytelling has consistently brought a contemporary female gaze into mainstream narratives without losing its spark.“The idea is to put forth real women and not the Madonna-whore complex,” Moitra says. “Women can be everything. One day you can actually be sacrificial, and the other day you can be ambitious, because that’s what real women are.”W-Power List 2026 | Meet India's Top Self-Made Women of 2026For filmmaker Karan Johar, who collaborated with Moitra on Rocky Aur Rani Kii Prem Kahaani (2023), her creative energy is unmistakable. “Ishita for me is a bona fide force of nature. She has an incredible sense of humour. She can bring pathos and humour to perfection… she creates a certain kind of magic in her writing, which I find very rare,” he tells Forbes India.Long before awards, film premieres and streaming success, Moitra discovered her writing skills through an essay competition in school. “During an essay competition in my 8th or 9th standard, the teacher was thrilled with what I wrote,” she recalls. “She read it out in front of the class. I realised that I love it [writing] and somehow it resonates with other people too.”That moment stayed with her. At a time when most career choices revolved around engineering or medicine, Moitra studied journalism at Delhi University before pursuing her master’s in mass communication at Jamia Millia Islamia. It was during those years that she realised fiction—and specifically screenwriting—was where she truly belonged.“That’s when I realised that what I wanted to do was write fiction… not novels, but writing for the big screen,” she says. “It was a journey that started at 13 and crystallised around 21.”Today, Moitra belongs to a generation of writers changing how women are written in Indian entertainment. Instead of presenting them through ideas of idealism, her characters exist in shades of grey, ambitious, insecure, loving, selfish, emotional and funny at once.That perspective has made her work resonate with the audiences, especially women who see themselves reflected in her characters. Moitra says much of her writing comes from observing people around her—friends, mothers, family conversations and everyday emotions. “I love going to family gatherings and hearing people talk,” she says. “Family gatherings are like gold mines for writers.”Along with her husband, filmmaker Neeraj Udhwani, Moitra co-created and co-produced Single Papa, a show exploring parenting and masculinity through a single father’s journey. This also marked a shift for her from being just a writer to co-creation and production. “When you are a writer otherwise, it’s like you’re just a teacher correcting papers,” she says with her characteristic humour. “Now [as a producer] I am the mother who is also the teacher.”Johar believes that emotional intelligence and shared cultural memory are central to Moitra’s voice as a writer. “I felt like we immediately connected as people, as friends and as collaborators,” he says. “Both of us have the same references from Hindi cinema. Both of us know the words of every single song that exists in the musicscape of Indian cinema.”Also Read: From small parts to centre stage, the rise of Chhaya KadamThat love for Hindi cinema runs deep for Moitra. “I’ve grown up watching masala Hindi films, and I love them. That is the film nutrition that I have been fed.”However, Moitra rejects the idea of writing differently for “mass” audiences. “I try to be authentic to the world of that story,” she says. “Sometimes when we start writing thinking what people expect from us, you can get a little muddled.”Motherhood has become one of the strongest anchors to shape Moitra’s perspective, changing the way she thinks about her work and legacy. “Whenever I do anything now, I think: Will my daughter like this? Is this something I want her to see and say, ‘My mama wrote this?’” she says. That grounding, she believes, becomes especially important in an industry dependent on constant validation. Family gives her perspective during both success and uncertainty.“Through my writing, I want to leave people with a smile on their faces and gratitude for relationships,” she says.

title: Microsoft Extends Windows 10 Extended Security Updates for Another Year Despite Pushing Users to Update to Windows 11
description: Microsoft continues to recommend migrating to Windows 11
content: Microsoft's consumer ESU programme for Windows 10 users was set to provide security updates until October this year. However, Microsoft has now quietly extended the programme by another year. As a result, eligible Windows 10 users will continue to receive security updates through October 12, 2027. An existing ESU license can be used to cover up to 10 devices.  Devices running on Windows 10, version 22H2 Home, Professional, Pro Education or Workstations edition are eligible to join the consumer Windows 10 ESU programme.

title: Video | Is the Skoda Enyaq vRS the ultimate one-car garage?
description:
Looking for the perfect blend of weekday skills and weekend thrills? This electric family SUV could be the answer

The perfect two-car garage. It’s a fantasy motoring enthusiasts have debated for decades.
Not familiar with the concept? It’s simple: if you had to choose two cars – one for everyday duties like commuting, the school run and shopping trips, and another purely for weekend B-road blasts – which would you choose? If you’re ever stuck for conversation with a fellow enthusiast, pose this question and watch the sparks fly.
But, alas, in today’s economic climate, the dream of running two cars – particularly when one is reserved almost entirely for Sundays – is becoming harder and harder to justify. And that means more and more drivers are starting to search for one car that’s capable of covering all bases: sensible when it needs to be, fun when it doesn’t.
Watch the video below as we explore whether the 335bhp all-electric Skoda Enyaq vRS really can deliver the best of both worlds.


Skoda Enyaq vRS: the electric family SUV that can do it all
There’s a particular genius to the vRS badge. Since the first Octavia vRS arrived in 2001, those three letters have stood for meaningful performance, real-world practicality and strong value. It’s the everyman performance car formula, refined over decades and now applied to Skoda’s electric family SUV flagship.
The Enyaq vRS continues that tradition with 335bhp, 697Nm and dual-motor all-wheel drive, delivering 0-62mph in 5.4 seconds. But it’s not just about straight-line pace. Dynamic Chassis Control, lowered suspension and revised steering calibration give it a level of composure and adjustability that no electric SUV this size has any right to, allowing it to switch between relaxed motorway cruiser and pulse-raising B-road blaster at the touch of a button.
Crucially, it remains an easy car to live with day to day. The 84kWh battery delivers up to 344 miles of range (WLTP), while 185kW DC charging enables a 10–80% top-up in around 26 minutes.
Inside, that signature vRS DNA injects sporty flair into an otherwise plush, minimalist cabin. There are vRS-specific sports seats, a bespoke sports steering wheel and contrast detailing. As you’d expect from a Škoda, equipment is generous, with a large 13in infotainment screen, augmented reality head-up display, heated seats and a 12-speaker CANTON sound system all included as standard. 
Practicality remains a core strength. A 585-litre boot, generous rear space and Skoda’s trademark ‘Smart, Spacious and Stylish’ touches – including an ice scraper in the tailgate and an umbrella in the door – ensure it can still deal with everything hectic family life throws at it without breaking a sweat. Maybe it’s time you try it for yourself.
Learn more about the Skoda Enyaq vRS

title: From small parts to centre stage, the rise of Chhaya Kadam
description: Chhaya Kadam finds it hard to erase 2001 from her memory. It was a year when she lost both her brother and father. “I felt as though my world had collapsed. I was confined to my home. I knew I should step out and carry on with life, but I was unable to get myself to do that,” says the actor who also underwent two knee surgeries after damaging a ligament the same year.Kadam, who had acted in a few plays in school, was not interested in taking to the stage in college. She had taken to kabaddi until her damaged knees meant she had to stop pursuing the sport. The tragedy in her family during the same period left her disillusioned and morose.It was around this time that she saw a newspaper advertisement about an acting workshop by theatre veteran Waman Kendre in Mumbai. Kadam believed it was a good opportunity to meet new people instead of spending time with relatives or friends, who, in all probability, would discuss the turmoil in her life. The course turned out to be a blessing for her.“I enjoyed the process… I got to learn a lot, and not just acting. It taught me to be independent and shaped me as a human. It made me understand that one does not become an actor merely by attending workshops or doing photo shoots. It erased all such assumptions. I realised I had to get better at many things—for instance, I didn’t read the newspapers, and I had language and diction issues because I hailed from Konkan. The workshop developed me in more ways than I could have imagined,” says Kadam, who has starred in acclaimed Marathi films like Sairat and Fandry, as well as recent noteworthy ones like the Hindi comedy-drama Laapataa Ladies, Sister Midnight and the Grand Prix (Cannes)-winning Malayalam film All We Imagine as Light.W-Power List 2026 | Meet India's Top Self-Made Women of 2026Kendre, who saw her from close quarters, describes her as a hardworking and passionate artiste. “It was evident that Chhaya would stand out. She had a lot of potential and was serious about her work. She is an honest actor,” the former director of the National School of Drama tells Forbes India. He adds that she trained for two-and-a-half months for his directorial venture and her first play, Zulwa, and ended up doing around 250 shows. That laid the foundation for her as an actor.As Kadam fell in love with acting during the workshop, she decided to pursue it as a profession. It was not easy, though, to make inroads for someone who did not come from a film family. Kadam’s father was a mill worker from Kalina, Mumbai, and her mother was a housewife. She was the youngest among five siblings—two brothers and three sisters.When the acting breaks did not come through, she took up smaller assignments such as working as an assistant for a Hindi serial. The hunt for proper parts continued. In fact, when she got to know that Doordarshan was making a short film, she approached the makers for a role. The director told her that casting was done and that they needed an assistant instead. She agreed, thinking it would help her make connections. The film was being shot in Baramati, Maharashtra, and as luck would have it, the role Kadam was vying for fell into her lap. The lady who was supposed to play the part did not turn up the day before her scene was scheduled to be filmed. The team asked Kadam to fill in, and she faced the camera for the first time—for Phatacha Pani (Marathi).“I struggled a lot in those five years after the workshop… in fact, I got my first play [Zulwa] in 2006. My family did not support me… they knew nothing about this industry or my battles. But all the obstacles that came my way during that phase made me stronger,” says Kadam. “I did everything with utmost sincerity and dedication. Even if I got one scene in a film, I accepted that and did justice to the part. But the industry is such that when you get work, it does not mean you have arrived. You have to keep looking for newer opportunities. And it is difficult to get those… it can take many years.”It indeed took a while for Kadam to stamp her authority with her performances. Today, the Madgaon Express actor is known for bringing authenticity to the characters she essays and for her nuanced approach to her craft. “With acting, you literally give birth to a person—their walk, mannerisms, body language, backstory, even if it’s a fictional character,” she explains. “I am an observant person. I study people. After I read what has been offered to me, I work on the small details and decorate the character.”Also Read: W-Power 2026: Why Huma Qureshi Chooses Reinvention Over ComfortTo play Suman Akka in Sairat, Kadam learnt Telugu from the camera crew on set, while in the case of Fandry, she took lessons in Kaikadi. “It is not enough to learn the lines. You need to know the language to understand the emotion,” she insists.According to Kendre, Kadam had an inferiority complex because of her economic status and language shortcomings. But she overcame those mental blocks and discovered herself as an actor and a human being. “Such a journey is rare. She has marched ahead confidently and has not looked back. I expect her to do a lot more,” he says.Achievements like going to Cannes for All We Imagine as Light are the icing on the cake for Kadam. She does not read too much into it though, saying it is in the past. “I like to live in the moment. I don’t think too far ahead. I just have to do good work,” says the actor, who likes to try various delicacies, travel to her native place in Konkan and spend time with the people closest to her.Conscious of the fact that she has to keep opening new doors for herself, Kadam appears unperturbed even if all this ends one day. “I came here with an empty bag. I have no fear about the bag being empty again,” she says.

title: Polymarket Reports $2.9 Million Theft, Says Customers Will Be Refunded
description: Compromised third-party dependency triggered phishing attack on users
content: Prediction market platform Polymarket has confirmed that users affected by a phishing attack stemming from a compromised third-party vendor will be fully refunded. Blockchain analyst Specter estimated that attackers stole nearly $2.94 million worth of cryptocurrency from at least 11 wallets after a malicious script was injected into the platform's website. Polymarket said the affected dependency has been removed and described the incident as isolated.

description: OnePlus N6 will feature an 8-megapixel selfie camera
content: OnePlus N6 will be launched in India with a flat display that refreshes at up to 120Hz and offers up to 1,200 nits peak brightness, the smartphone maker revealed on Friday. Moreover, the smartphone will feature a dual rear camera system, headlined by a 50-megapixel primary shooter and an unspecified secondary camera. On the front, the OnePlus N6 will be equipped with an 8-megapixel camera for selfies and video calls. The company claims that the handset will be capable of recording videos at up to 60 fps, while also offering Dual-View video mode. For thermal management, the OnePlus N6 will launch equipped with a vapour chamber cooling solution, offering a 5,300 sq mm heat dissipation area.

The handset sports a 6.75-inch HD+ (720 x 1,570 pixels) LCD touchscreen, offering up to 120Hz refresh rate, up to 1,200 nits peak brightness, 256 ppi pixel density, 240Hz touch sampling rate, 83 percent NTSC, and 16.7 million colours. title: Ketan Agrawal murder case : केतन अग्रवाल प्रकरणात आता उज्ज्वल निकम यांची एन्ट्री, फडणवीसांचा मोठा निर्णय
description: मुंबई । Mumbai राज्यभरात प्रचंड खळबळ उडवून देणाऱ्या लोहगड (पुणे) येथील केतन अग्रवाल हत्या प्रकरणाला आता एक नवे वळण मिळाले आहे. ‘प्रेम, लग्न आणि धोका’ अशा चक्रव्यूहात अडकलेल्या या गुन्ह्यामुळे पुरुषांवरील अत्याचाराची आणि हल्ल्यांची एक भयानक दुसरी बाजू समाजासमोर आली आहे. आपल्या प्रियकरासोबत कट रचून, होणाऱ्या नवऱ्याला अत्यंत निर्दयीपणे संपवल्याच्या आरोपाखाली मुख्य आरोपी सिया गोयल […]
content:
मुंबई । Mumbai



राज्यभरात प्रचंड खळबळ उडवून देणाऱ्या लोहगड (पुणे) येथील केतन अग्रवाल हत्या प्रकरणाला आता एक नवे वळण मिळाले आहे. ‘प्रेम, लग्न आणि धोका’ अशा चक्रव्यूहात अडकलेल्या या गुन्ह्यामुळे पुरुषांवरील अत्याचाराची आणि हल्ल्यांची एक भयानक दुसरी बाजू समाजासमोर आली आहे.



आपल्या प्रियकरासोबत कट रचून, होणाऱ्या नवऱ्याला अत्यंत निर्दयीपणे संपवल्याच्या आरोपाखाली मुख्य आरोपी सिया गोयल आणि तिचा प्रियकर चेतन सध्या पोलिसांच्या कोठडीत आहेत. पुणे पोलिसांकडून या प्रकरणाची कसून चौकशी सुरू असून दररोज थक्क करणारे खुलासे समोर येत आहेत.



मिळालेल्या माहितीनुसार, पुणे पोलिसांनी आता आपला मोर्चा आरोपी सिया गोयलच्या कुटुंबाकडे वळवला आहे. या संपूर्ण कटात कुटुंबातील कोणाचा सहभाग होता का, याचा शोध घेण्यासाठी पोलिसांनी थेट सियाच्या भावाला चौकशीसाठी ताब्यात घेतले आहे. त्यामुळे या प्रकरणात आगामी काळात आणखी काही मोठे खुलासे होण्याची शक्यता वर्तवली जात आहे.



दुसरीकडे, आपल्या तरुण मुलाला गमावलेल्या अग्रवाल कुटुंबावर दुःखाचा डोंगर कोसळला आहे. केतन अग्रवाल यांचे वडील विशाल अग्रवाल यांनी आज पुण्यात राज्याचे मुख्यमंत्री देवेंद्र फडणवीस यांची भेट घेतली. आपल्या मुलाला लवकरात लवकर न्याय मिळावा आणि आरोपींना कठोर शिक्षा व्हावी, अशी आर्त हाक त्यांनी मुख्यमंत्र्यांकडे दिली. यावर मुख्यमंत्र्यांनी कुटुंबाचे सांत्वन करत, “दोषींना कोणत्याही परिस्थितीत सोडले जाणार नाही, सरकार पीडित कुटुंबाला न्याय देण्यासाठी कटिबद्ध आहे,” असे आश्वासन दिले.



पीडित कुटुंबाची संवेदनशीलता आणि प्रकरणाचे गांभीर्य लक्षात घेऊन हा खटला ‘फास्ट ट्रॅक कोर्टात’ (जलदगती न्यायालय) चालवण्याची आणि राज्याचे ज्येष्ठ विधिज्ञ उज्ज्वल निकम यांची ‘विशेष सरकारी वकील’ म्हणून नियुक्ती करण्याची मागणी विशाल अग्रवाल यांनी केली होती. मुख्यमंत्री देवेंद्र फडणवीस यांनी ही मागणी तातडीने मान्य करत विधी व न्याय विभागाच्या सचिवांना तसे निर्देश दिले आहेत. विशेष म्हणजे, ज्येष्ठ विधिज्ञ उज्ज्वल निकम यांनीही या प्रकरणात विशेष सरकारी वकील म्हणून काम पाहण्यास आपली संमती दर्शवली आहे.



या धक्कादायक घटनेमुळे सियाच्या वडिलांना मोठा मानसिक धक्का बसला असून ते रुग्णालयात उपचार घेत आहेत. कालच त्यांची प्रकृती स्थिर झाल्याने त्यांना अतिदक्षता विभागातून (ICU) जनरल वॉर्डमध्ये हलवण्यात आले. माध्यमांशी बोलताना त्यांनी एक मोठा खुलासा केला. सियावर या लग्नासाठी घरून कोणताही दबाव नव्हता. केतनचे स्थळ आले तेव्हा ती खूप आनंदी होती आणि तिने स्वतःच्या मर्जीनेच केतनला पसंत केले होते, असे तिच्या वडिलांनी सांगितले. जर तिचे कोणावर प्रेम होते, तर तिने लग्नाला नकार का दिला नाही? आणि थेट हत्येचा कट का रचला? या प्रश्नांनी आता संपूर्ण तपास यंत्रणेला चक्रावून सोडले आहे.

title: Rocket Report: China may soon attempt booster landing; Rocket Lab does rapid response
description: Is SpaceX planning to end its Transporter program?
content:
Welcome to Edition 8.47 of the Rocket Report! We have now very nearly reached the midpoint of 2026, a year in which several new US rockets were advertised as potentially making their debuts. But now, we have to wonder whether any of them—Rocket Lab's Neutron, Stoke Space's Nova, Relativity Space's Terran R, and Astra's Rocket 4—will make it. I'd probably put the over/under at something like 0.5 of these launching. Please share your thoughts in the comments below!
As always, we welcome reader submissions, and if you don't want to miss an issue, please subscribe using the box below (the form will not appear on AMP-enabled versions of the site). Each report will include information on small-, medium-, and heavy-lift rockets as well as a quick look ahead at the next three launches on the calendar.

    
                        
                  
      

Rocket Lab executes rapid response mission. Last Friday Rocket Lab launched the Victus Haze mission just 16 hours and 42 minutes after receiving the US Space Force’s Notice to Launch, beating the previous record by more than 10 hours, the company said. The launch was scarcely announced in advance, Ars reports. The only public indication of an impending launch was the release of a warning for pilots and sailors to steer clear of the rocket’s flight path. Rocket Lab did not provide a livestream of the launch, as it does for most of its missions.Read full article

title: Next-generation Hyundai Elantra brings striking new look
description:
New Elantra adopts 'Art of Steel' design principles and comes with a choice of petrol or hybrid

Hyundai has revealed the next iteration of its Elantra saloon, which brings a dramatic new look.
Named the Avante in its home market of South Korea, it is the most extreme example of the brand’s new 'Art of Steel' design language yet, with a number of sharp lines and aggressive angles across its bodywork.
It is slightly larger than the rival Skoda Octavia, owing to a 30mm increase in its wheelbase compared with the previous-generation Elantra. The saloon has also grown 30mm wider and Hyundai said it has prioritised improving interior space. 
Inside, it has been designed to be “cozy and comfortable”, according to Hyundai. The dashboard and door cards’ designs cocoon around the seats, with customisable LED backlighting. 
As in the recently revealed Ioniq 3 hatchback, the driver’s display is set high on the dashboard. This is intended to keep important information in the driver’s line of sight, so they do not have to divert their attention away from the road to check their speed or remaining range. 
A large infotainment touchscreen, measuring either 12.9in or 14.6in, takes centre stage. It runs on Hyundai’s new Pleos operating system, which will allow new functionality such as installing navigation and music apps in the screen, rather than having to use smartphone mirroring. An array of switches and dials for the climate control system sits just below the main screen.

In South Korea, it will be available with two powertrains: a 2.0-litre petrol four-cylinder with 147bhp and a 1.6-litre hybrid with 155bhp. 
The hybrid also brings a new predictive regenerative braking system that reads the road ahead and automatically adjusts the strength of the regenerative effect, helping to keep the battery topped up.
In addition, it gets a ‘stay mode’ that allows the small traction battery’s reserves to power the climate controls and infotainment system while parked, in similar fashion to an EV with a larger battery.
South Korean deliveries of the new Elantra will begin in the coming weeks, although Hyundai has yet to confirm whether it will be sold in mainland Europe or the UK. The previous-generation car was not made available in those regions, despite having been tested there. However, Autocar understands that it has not been ruled out for the latest iteration, suggesting Hyundai may have had a change of heart.
It also remains to be seen whether the high-performance Elantra N will return – and whether it too could make it to Europe.

title: Operation Sindoor: ऑपरेशन सिंदूरमधील शहीदांची नावे पहिल्यांदाच जाहीर; राष्ट्रीय युद्ध स्मारकाच्या ‘रोल ऑफ ऑनर’ यादीत समावेश
description: नवी दिल्ली वृत्तसंस्था | New Delhi‘ऑपरेशन सिंदूर’मध्येभारतीय जवानांनी भीमपराक्रम गाजवत पाकिस्तानला त्याची जागा दाखवून दिली. भारतीय जवानांच्या आक्रमणासमोर पाकिस्तानने अक्षरशः गुडघे टेकले आणि युद्धविरामाची अक्षरश: भीक मागितली. या ऑपरेशनदरम्यान आपल्या प्राणाची पर्वा न करता लढलेल्या ६ जवानांना हौतात्म्य आले. या शहीदांची नावे पहिल्यांदाच अधिकृतपणे जाहीर करण्यात आली असून त्यांना वीरता पुरस्काराने सन्मानित करण्यात आले आहे. […]
content:
नवी दिल्ली वृत्तसंस्था | New Delhi‘ऑपरेशन सिंदूर’मध्येभारतीय जवानांनी भीमपराक्रम गाजवत पाकिस्तानला त्याची जागा दाखवून दिली. भारतीय जवानांच्या आक्रमणासमोर पाकिस्तानने अक्षरशः गुडघे टेकले आणि युद्धविरामाची अक्षरश: भीक मागितली. या ऑपरेशनदरम्यान आपल्या प्राणाची पर्वा न करता लढलेल्या ६ जवानांना हौतात्म्य आले. या शहीदांची नावे पहिल्यांदाच अधिकृतपणे जाहीर करण्यात आली असून त्यांना वीरता पुरस्काराने सन्मानित करण्यात आले आहे.



रायफलमॅन सुनील कुमार यांना मरणोत्तर ‘वीर चक्र’ प्रदान करून सन्मानित करण्यात आले तर सार्जंट सुरेंद्र कुमार यांना ‘वायु पदक’ प्रदान करून सन्मानित करण्यात आले आहे. देशासाठी सर्वोच्च बलिदान देणाऱ्या या वीरांमध्ये, सुभेदार मेजर पवन कुमार, रायफलमॅन सुनील कुमार, लांस नायक दिनेश कुमार, अग्निवीर मूळ मुरली नायक, हवालदार सुनील कुमार सिंह आणि सार्जेंट सुरेंद्र कुमार यांचा समावेश आहे. संरक्षण सूत्रांनुसार, यातील बहुतेक जवान जम्मू आणि काश्मीरमधील मोहिमेदरम्यान शहीद झाले.




Indian military has updated India's National War Memorial with all military casualties of Op Sindoor.Meanwhile Pakistani military in an effort to hide its huge casulaties in war with Taliban, Baloch rebels as well as LoC skirmishes from 2016-2020, has turned down Pak PMO… https://t.co/SA1SjdnlFW pic.twitter.com/jcsCupy0kj— WLVN (@TheLegateIN) June 25, 2026




मिळालेल्या माहितीनुसार, भारत सरकारने ऑपरेशन सिंदूरमध्ये राष्ट्रासाठी सर्वोच्च बलिदान देणाऱ्या सहा शूर जवानांची नावे प्रथमच जाहीर केली आहेत. या सर्व शहीदांसाठी दिल्लीतील राष्ट्रीय युद्ध स्मारकात एक जागा निश्चित करण्यात आली आहे, जिथे त्यांची नावे विशेष ग्रॅनाइट विटांवर कायमस्वरूपी कोरली जातील. या सहा शहीदांमध्ये भारतीय लष्कराचे पाच जवान आणि भारतीय हवाई दलाचे एक सार्जंट यांचा समावेश आहे.



Ram Mandir Donation Case: राम मंदिराच्या दान चोरी प्रकरणी मोठी घडामोड; चंपत राय, अनिल मिश्रांचा तडकाफडकी राजीनामा



यांपैकी, सुभेदार मेजर पवन कुमार हे १० इन्फंट्री ब्रिगेड मुख्यालयात, रायफलमॅन सुनील कुमार हे ४ जम्मू-काश्मीर लाइट इन्फंट्रीत, लांस नायक दिनेश कुमार हे ५ फील्ड रेजिमेंटमध्ये, अग्निवीर मूल मुरली नायक हे ८५१ लाइट रेजिमेंटमध्ये, हवालदार सुनील कुमार सिंह हे २३७ फील्ड वर्कशॉपमध्ये आणि सार्जेंट सुरेंद्र कुमार हे भारतीय हवाई दलाच्या ३९ विंगमध्ये तैनात होते. या सर्व हुतात्म्यांची नावे नॅशनल वॉर मेमोरियलच्या वेबसाईटवरही जोडण्यात आली आहेत. ‘त्याग चक्र’मधील एका विशेष विटेवरही त्यांची नावे लिहिली जातील. राष्ट्रपती द्रौपदी मुर्मू यांनी रायफलमॅन सुनील कुमार यांच्या कुटुंबीयांना मरणोत्तर वीर चक्र प्रदान केले.

title: Underweight Deliveries, Heavy Questions As Consumers Raise Red Flags
description: Deceiving to sell: Small weight discrepancies, missing product details, and recurring consumer complaints expose growing transparency gaps in quick commerce, raising questions about accountability and fair business practices.
content: Deceiving to sell: Convenience has quietly become an addiction. Why step out for everyday essentials when a quick commerce app can drop them at your door in minutes? But in the rush to make life easier, there is something we are not noticing — in the name of convenience, we may be buying health hazards too.That is exactly why Times Now Digital is running a consumer rights protection campaign, asking quick commerce platforms to be fair and transparent with their customers. One of the most hazardous and unethical practices we are flagging is the failure to clearly display the manufacturing and expiry dates of perishable products, among other lapses. Times Now Digital reached out to Zepto, Blinkit, Instamart, Flipkart Minutes, and Amazon to understand why manufacturing and expiry dates are not displayed on their platforms. None of them responded. On a basic consumer-safety question — what's the manufacturing date? When does it expire? So far, there has been no response to these basic consumer-safety questions. The campaign's core focus is transparency — and a big part of transparency comes down to a simple test: what you ordered versus what you get.We got in touch with some loyal customers of quick commerce platforms to find out whether they had run into transparency issues, recently or in the past, and whether those problems kept recurring. One regular user pointed to what they described as another major concern.In a recent incident under our investigation, the customer from the Delhi NCR area ordered vegetables from Zepto. When the order arrived, they grew curious because the carrots looked lighter than expected. On weighing them, they found the quantity was lower than what was listed on the platform: the carrots were supposed to weigh between 200 and 300 grams, but the package came in at just 196 grams.Although the discrepancy was not large, it was enough to raise concern. The customer then weighed the other vegetable packs in the order, and to their surprise, every one weighed less than the quantity listed on the platform.According to the customer, a pack of onions listed at between 900 grams and 1 kilogram weighed 859 grams. A pack of tomatoes listed at 500 grams weighed 460 grams, while a cucumber pack listed at between 500 and 600 grams weighed 490 grams.                          While the differences may appear small on a single order, the customer questioned whether consumers are consistently receiving the quantities they pay for and whether greater transparency is needed from quick commerce platforms.Issue Raised With The Zepto Support TeamWhen the issue was raised with the platform, and after several exchanges of messages and images, another problem came to light: the customer was unable to upload images directly to the platform due to an error. This too was flagged to the support executive, which ultimately led the customer to email the images to the Zepto support team.                          After a long discussion lasting about 25 minutes, the company offered a refund of Rs 45. The customer repeatedly questioned how a delivery in which the weight of the vegetables did not match the weight listed on the website could be settled with a refund alone. Not everybody is going to weigh their vegetables every day, the customer argued, and so such discrepancies may go unnoticed. The customer further pointed out that the issue was not limited to a single packet of vegetables.Despite these concerns, the support team maintained that it could only provide a refund and said the matter would be escalated to “higher authorities”.This is not the first time a quick commerce customer has faced such an issue. Several people have come forward on social media with similar complaints about the weight of fruits and vegetables on other platforms like Swiggy Instamart and Blinkit, among others.                        Similar Incidents Against Quick Comms Raised In PastPreviously, one customer shared his frustration with Blinkit on Reddit, alleging that he had received significantly less than the quantity he had paid for. He shared an image of a grape order and claimed the package weighed less than the amount listed on the platform, adding that he had encountered similar issues with Blinkit deliveries before. The user claimed that a 500 gm pack of grapes weighed just 395 grams, including the packaging.              In another incident, a Reddit user claimed that Swiggy Instamart had delivered underweight vegetables, sharing photos and screenshots of conversations with customer support. According to the user, several items in the order weighed less than the quantities listed on the platform: a cauliflower listed at 400–600 grams weighed just 145 grams, while a 250-gram capsicum pack weighed 170 grams and a 1-kg potato order weighed 965 grams.                          Pls check your Blinkit deliveries byu/Jusklickin inindiaGovernment Mandates For Quick Commerce PlatformsThe growing number of complaints about underweight fruit and vegetable deliveries raises serious questions about transparency and accountability in the quick commerce sector. While individual discrepancies may seem minor, but consumers argue that even small shortfalls can add up over time. And these concerns are not new.For at least the past two years, regulators such as the Central Consumer Protection Authority (CCPA) and the Food Safety and Standards Authority of India (FSSAI) have urged e-commerce and quick commerce platforms to clearly display key consumer information, including expiry dates, accurate product weights and other essential details. Despite these directives, transparency-related issues continue to surface. The FSSAI recently said it had issued more than two dozen notices to e-commerce Food Business Operators (FBOs) in recent weeks.Yet many consumers still say they do not receive complete information, or the quantity they paid for, raising fresh questions about how effectively the rules are being enforced across the sector.A few grams of carrots. A Rs 45 refund. Easy enough to shrug off, until you multiply it across millions of orders, every single day. Convenience may be arriving at our doorstep in minutes, but the question worth weighing is this: how much are we quietly handing over in return?

title: Callum Design reinvents Jaguar XJ220 for new era
description:
Ian Callum's design consultancy is poised to reveal its take on the legendary supercar

The eponymous consultancy of acclaimed car designer Ian Callum has reinvented the Jaguar XJ220. 
The new take on the XJ220 has yet to be revealed in full, but a side-profile image published by Callum Design showcases a dramatically different styling direction compared with the original. 
It is notably sharper around its haunches and there is a greater slope at its rear end. 
However, several cues remain intact, including the egg-shaped side windows and air ducts along its flanks.
Callum Design described the reimagined XJ220 as a “concept” and a “design study”, emphasising that there are no concrete plans to build a road-going car. But it added that the project “shows what’s possible” when working with the company, suggesting that a client with enough cash to spend could commission their own car.
The reworked XJ220 is expected to be revealed in full in the coming months. October marks the 35th anniversary of the production XJ220’s unveiling at the Tokyo motor show and that could provide Callum Design a fitting opportunity to celebrate the supercar.
Ian Callum, who was director of design at Jaguar from 1999 to 2019, has in recent years put his own spin on several historic cars. These include the Wood and Pickett Mini and the Aston Martin Vanquish – the latter of which he also originally designed. His consultancy has also been responsible for building a road-legal version of the Jaguar C-X75 concept, the ill-fated successor to the XJ220 and a car originally created while he was Jaguar's design chief.

title: Renault 4 and 5 due extra power and range in 2027
description:
New ‘Gen 2 Evo’ motors made their debut with the Twingo but will be soon be rolled out in larger cars

The Renault 4 and 5 will be updated next year, bringing more efficient and more powerful motors.
The R5 arrived in 2024, and it was followed by the R4, its crossover twin, in early 2025. Both models are based on the AmpR Small platform and use Renault’s second-generation externally excited-synchronous (EESM) motors, which are available in two configurations. 
The R5 Urban Range pairs a 121bhp EESM with a 40kWh battery, while the Comfort Range gets a 148bhp motor and a 52kWh battery pack. In the UK, the larger R4 is only available with the Comfort Range set-up.
These powertrains enable the R5 to deliver a range of either 192 or 252 miles. The latter figure is reduced to 250 miles in the chunkier R4.

Renault is now ready to roll out updated ‘Gen 2 Evo’ motors for the two models, drawing on its learnings from developing third-generation drive units for the next Scenic and Megane that are due in 2028.
“We are still working on how to improve the efficiency and power [of the Gen 2 motors],” said Marianne Bataillon, Renault’s director of EV motor and battery development. “We are going to apply these [changes] at the end of 2026 or beginning of 2027.”
Bataillon highlighted the motor’s inverter and reducers as points of improvement that have netting greater efficiency and improved power outputs.
The new motors will help to keep the R4 and R5 on a par with newer rivals on technical terms. The incoming Volkswagen ID Polo outdoes the R5 in the range stakes, offering either 204 or 283 miles between charges, depending on which powertrain it is fitted with. The same is true of the new Skoda Epiq: it yields either 190 or 272 miles of range, with the higher figure beating the R4.
Renault’s new Gen 2 Evo motor made its debut in the Twingo, which arrives in the UK at the end of this year. In that car, however, its output is reduced to 80bhp, owing to its city-focused remit.

title: Weather tracker: Heatwave expected to bring Germany and Poland to 40C
description: Austrian Grand Prix declares heat hazard as dramatic rise in temperatures forecast in central and northern EuropeTemperatures are forecast to rise dramatically in parts of central and northern Europe this weekend as the intense heatwave continues. In Germany and Poland, highs up to or exceeding 40C (104F) are expected on both Saturday and Sunday, days after swathes of France experienced similar extreme temperatures. The Austrian Grand Prix, taking place this weekend in Spielberg, has declared a heat hazard, the first race to do so this season. Temperatures are expected to rise into the low 30s celsius during the race, almost 10C warmer than usual for the venue.Across Europe, the intense heat has led to severe thunderstorms. In the Sverdlovsk region of central Russia, a strong tornado was reported on the evening of 22 June. Rated a 3 out of 5 on the international Fujita scale, the twister injured 16 people and destroyed or damaged about 100 homes and businesses in the town of Kushva. Emergency services have been working on restoring power to the town. Tornadoes are not unheard of in Russia but are rare. Forecasters say they may become more common in future as the climate breaks down. Storm warnings remained in effect in the southern part of the country on Friday. Continue reading...

title: Meet Micron, the under-the-radar chipmaker that just reported a 346% sales surge and helped stop a global AI selloff
description: The company behind the memory chips powering AI started in a dentist’s basement in Boise.
content:
Micron may not be a household name, but it may just be among the most important tech companies in the AI era, and definitely one with a surprising backstory. 



On Thursday, the memory chipmaker reported an eye-popping 346% surge in quarterly revenue. As if that weren’t enough, it reported a profit of $28.2 billion for the quarter, almost 15 times as high as the same quarter last year. By all accounts, the company blew past analyst expectations, sending its stock soaring almost 16% in after-hours trading. The rally continued into Thursday evening, with Micron’s shares closing up 15% at $1,213 per share.



Micron’s stellar results have helped stabilize markets after a bruising selloff in AI and tech-related stocks earlier this week by pushing up futures for both the tech-heavy Nasdaq and the broader S&P 500 indexes. It has also given Wall Street hope that the AI trade still has some steam.



“This shows the memory and chip trade is well-intact and still in the early stages of playing out with the AI Revolution still in the third inning,” wrote tech bull and Wedbush analyst Dan Ives in a Wednesday note. 



Today, Micron boasts a $1.3 trillion market cap and is one of the three biggest computer memory manufacturers in the world, alongside Samsung and SK Hynix, and the only major one based in the U.S. But far from a perennial sure bet, Micron comes from humble roots and took the long way to success.



And that’s evident in its business model. While Micron’s business is anything but flashy, it makes something essential: the memory chips that store and move data at blistering speeds inside AI servers. 



High-bandwidth memory chips like those made by Micron help AI processors function by stacking multiple layers of memory directly on top of each other and connecting them with microscopic wiring that allows data to flow at extremely high speeds.



Because training a large language model requires moving billions of data points per second between processors and memory, the chips Micron makes need to be fast enough to keep up.



Micron’s story



The company, which today has more than 50,000 employees, was founded in 1978 in the basement of a Boise dental office. Twin brothers Ward and Joe Parkinson were two of the four founders. Joe was a lawyer in Boise, while Ward, after earning a master’s degree in computer design from Stanford, worked at the Dallas-based Mostek Corp., which at the time was one of the dominant players in dynamic random-access memory, or DRAM. 



After he left the company, Ward teamed up with two of his colleagues from Mostek, Dennis Wilson and Doug Pitman, to create Micron. When they returned to Idaho at the request of Pitman, Joe helped them draft incorporation papers for the new company. The foursome found a good deal on a space beneath a dentist’s office, where “we got used to smelling happy gas,” Joe Parkinson told the Idaho Statesman, and the company was born.



Their early funding of $300,000 came from a handful of local businessmen persuaded over lunch at a downtown restaurant, and later from J.R. Simplot, a potato baron from Idaho, according to the outlet. 



The fledgling company’s first contract was for Mostek, the very company three of the four cofounders had left. Mostek wanted Micron to help it design a 64K memory chip, which at the time was cutting-edge technology—but today would hold only enough memory for a single short email. 



Micron took on the challenge and improved on the existing design of the 64K DRAM chip to make it the smallest in the world, setting Micron on a course to mass-produce its own improved 64K chips starting in 1981—arguably its first step to success.



Today, Micron has manufacturing facilities abroad, including in Taiwan and Japan, and is rapidly expanding its capabilities domestically thanks to the 2022 CHIPS Act, which provided billions of dollars in funding to bolster the U.S. chipmaking industry. Yet in 1980, the company had only one manufacturing facility on desert land southeast of Boise, owned by one of the company’s early investors, according to the Idaho Statesman. 



It was this manufacturing facility, which still stands today, that helped Micron mass-produce its 64K memory chip product that was then used in many early computers including the Commodore 64 home computer. 



As the company grew, it began producing improved chips with more memory, from 64K to 256K to its 1-megabit memory chip, which had about 16 times the memory of the company’s original 64K chip. By 1998, the company had purchased Texas Instruments’ worldwide memory operations, making it one of the biggest memory producers in the world.



Surviving fierce competition



But Micron’s rise was never linear. Throughout the 1980s and 1990s, the company faced an existential threat from Japanese chipmakers, backed by government subsidies, that flooded the market with cheap memory chips, slashing prices below the cost of production. While smaller American rivals like Intel and Advanced Micro Devices (AMD) were forced to exit the memory chip market because of competition, Micron survived partly through aggressive cost-cutting, antidumping lawsuits, and its efforts to convince U.S. government officials that Japanese rivals were violating international trade rules with their low-cost chips. This pressure eventually helped bring about the landmark 1986 U.S.-Japan Semiconductor Agreement, which set minimum price floors for Japanese memory chips sold in the U.S.



But partly because of competition, for years memory chips were a commodity business with prices that swung wildly. Some years brought large profits, while other years nearly brought the company to an end as the market produced more memory chips than clients needed.



Micron went public on the Nasdaq in 1984 at $13 per share, far below today’s price of more than $1,200 per share. For decades, Micron’s boom-and-bust business made Wall Street view the company as a short-term trade rather than a long-term investment.



Nevertheless, as the global memory market consolidated into the hands of three companies, Micron, Samsung, and SK Hynix, Micron’s business became much more reliable. These three companies now control about 95% of global DRAM production.



The rise of artificial intelligence has now taken the company’s memory business to a new level. Micron’s newfound dominance is partly the result of a new category of memory chip that the company has capitalized on. High-bandwidth memory, or HBM, is a specialized product that stacks memory chips on top of each other to move data at extraordinary speeds—exactly what AI processors need to function. 



The company’s HBM4 chip, its most advanced product, released in March, is designed specifically for Nvidia’s Vera Rubin platform, which helps power major AI systems like ChatGPT.



Yet only a handful of companies have the capacity to mass-produce these top-end products. Unlike traditional DRAM chips, HBM chips are extraordinarily complex to manufacture, and there is a shortage of supply worldwide.



Analysts at investment bank William Blair said it’s this dynamic that assures Micron will be an essential company in the AI race for years to come.



“Micron remains one of the key beneficiaries of the AI supercycle, which is consuming a growing share of the global memory capacity and driving record-high prices and profits for vendors,” the analysts wrote in a Wednesday note.
This story was originally featured on Fortune.com
